skill.name: amazon-best-selling-products-finder-api-skill
skill.description: Extract Amazon best-selling product data through the BrowserAct API, including titles, prices, ratings, reviews, sales signals, availability, promotions, ASINs, categories, badges, and product links.
skill.summary: Find Amazon best-selling products and return structured marketplace data through BrowserAct.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static findings are partly confirmed: the skill intentionally reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends authenticated requests to the BrowserAct API. The Markdown backtick, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and critical combined-pattern alerts are false positives, but the community skill still warrants a medium risk warning because it executes a local script with user-provided arguments and transmits search inputs to a third-party API.

medium_findings.title: BrowserAct API Key Sent to Third-Party Service
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and places it in an Authorization header for requests to the BrowserAct API. This appears intentional for the advertised API integration, but it exposes a secret to a third-party service and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads the BrowserAct API key and sends it as a bearer token to the configured API host. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the concern is disclosure and dependency risk rather than confirmed theft.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Executed Script Uses User-Controlled Arguments
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The skill instructs the agent to run a Python command using search keywords, limit, and marketplace URL supplied by the user. The Python script does not invoke a shell, but an agent that builds the shell command unsafely could introduce command injection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static command findings in SKILL.md are mostly Markdown backticks, but the documented invocation does combine user-provided values with a terminal command. Risk depends on how the host agent quotes arguments.

medium_findings.title: User-Provided Marketplace URL Forwarded to Remote Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script accepts a marketplace URL from command-line input and forwards it to the BrowserAct workflow without validation. This supports regional Amazon marketplaces, but it may let users direct the remote automation workflow to unexpected domains.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marketplace_url value is clearly user-controlled and sent to the external API. The impact is limited because browsing occurs through BrowserAct, but the lack of allowlist is real.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Most external command alerts in SKILL.md point to Markdown inline code, parameter names, output field names, or a fenced command example. Only the documented Python command is operational guidance; the rest are not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown formatting and examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No code in SKILL.md can execute by itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited lines discuss API keys and product data, not hashing, encryption, or weak algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the referenced lines found no cryptographic API usage. The scanner appears to have matched key-related words rather than actual cryptography.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE FOR BLOCKING CONCERNS: The reviewed skill text does not contain instructions to override system messages, skip security review, claim pre-approval, or change evaluator behavior. No evidence found of prompt injection in the analyzed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains agent usage instructions for the skill, but not suspicious evaluator-targeting language. Confidence is high for the reviewed file set.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Network Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The script sends HTTP POST and GET requests to the BrowserAct API using an Authorization bearer token. This is required for the skill, but it is a sensitive network pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requests and Authorization header are explicit in the script. The pattern is sensitive even though the destination matches the advertised service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Secret Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment and stops if it is missing. This is expected but should be visible to users before installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable name appears in both metadata and script logic. It is a normal API credential pattern, not hidden access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Long-Running Remote Workflow Polling
dangerous_patterns.description: The script starts a remote workflow and polls task status for up to 300 seconds. This can consume API quota and should be run only after the user confirms the query parameters.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The polling loop and documented long-running behavior are clear. The security impact is operational cost and user consent rather than direct compromise.

content.value_statement: Amazon marketplace research can be slow when product details must be copied by hand. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow that returns structured best-selling product data for a keyword and marketplace.
actual_capabilities: Runs the included Python script against the BrowserAct workflow API.
Accepts a search keyword, product limit, and Amazon marketplace URL.
Polls the remote task and reports timestamped status updates.
Returns product fields such as title, price, rating, reviews, ASIN, badge, and product URL.
Uses BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the local environment for authentication.
Provides retry guidance for failed tasks and invalid authorization errors.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key and network access.
Sends query parameters and the API credential to BrowserAct.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and Amazon page behavior.
The script may time out before returning results because the current polling logic returns after the loop.

use_cases.title: Validate Product Demand
use_cases.description: Collect top product listings, ratings, and review counts for a candidate keyword before sourcing inventory.

use_cases.title: Compare Regional Marketplaces
use_cases.description: Run the same product search on different Amazon marketplace URLs to compare price and availability signals.

use_cases.title: Track Competitor Listings
use_cases.description: Gather current prices, promotions, badges, and sales indicators for competitor products in a category.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small data pull.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top 10 best-selling Amazon products for "wireless keyboard" on https://www.amazon.com and summarize prices, ratings, and review counts.

prompt_templates.title: Category Opportunity Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare product demand for a new product idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for "travel coffee mug" with a limit of 20. Highlight recurring brands, price ranges, review strength, and promotion patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Regional Marketplace Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze availability and pricing across regions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run best-seller searches for "running shoes" on amazon.com and amazon.co.uk. Compare the product mix, badges, prices, and availability signals.

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Monitoring Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect up to 30 Amazon results for "portable blender". Group findings by brand, identify strong competitors, and flag products with high sales signals or promotions.

output_examples.input: Find best-selling wireless keyboards on Amazon US.
output_examples.output: A ranked product summary with titles, brands, current prices, star ratings, review counts, availability, badges, and product links.

output_examples.input: Compare running shoes on Amazon US and UK.
output: Regional comparison of visible price ranges.
Notes on shared and unique brands.
Availability and badge differences for each marketplace.

output_examples.input: Research portable blender competitors with a limit of 30.
output_examples.output: A competitor brief covering common price bands, review strength, promotions, sales indicators, and ASINs for follow-up research.

best_practices: Confirm the keyword, result limit, and marketplace URL before running the script.
Use a small limit first to verify the query and API key.
Treat returned marketplace data as time-sensitive and record when it was collected.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into prompts or command history when an environment variable is available.
Do not run broad repeated searches without checking BrowserAct quota and cost.
Do not assume a product is profitable from best-seller data alone.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment before the script can run.

faq.question: What data can it return?
faq.answer: It can return product titles, brands, prices, ratings, review counts, sales signals, availability, promotions, ASINs, categories, badges, and URLs.

faq.question: Can it search non-US Amazon marketplaces?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill accepts a marketplace URL, such as Amazon UK or Amazon Germany, when supported by the BrowserAct workflow.

faq.question: Does it scrape Amazon directly from my machine?
faq.answer: No. The local script calls the BrowserAct API, and BrowserAct runs the remote workflow.

faq.question: Is the returned data real-time?
faq.answer: The data reflects the workflow result at run time, but marketplace listings and prices can change quickly.

faq.question: What should I do if authorization fails?
faq.answer: Check that BROWSERACT_API_KEY is set, valid, and not expired. Do not retry with an invalid key.
